name: plugins
context: Tailwind plugins — official plugins, custom utilities, custom variants, @plugin directive, and the v3/v4 plugin API
info: Tailwind's plugin system extends the framework with new utilities, components, base styles, and variants. Official first-party plugins include @tailwindcss/typography (prose classes for rendered markdown/HTML), @tailwindcss/forms (form element reset), and @tailwindcss/container-queries (now built-in to v4). In v4, many plugin use cases are replaced by CSS-native directives: @utility for custom utilities, @custom-variant for custom variants, and @theme for design tokens. The @plugin directive loads legacy JS plugins. This node teaches agents when to use plugins vs CSS directives, how to create custom utilities and variants, and how to integrate official plugins.

sections.title: @tailwindcss/typography plugin
sections.body: Adds `prose` class for rich text styling. Install: `npm install @tailwindcss/typography`. Load: `@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography"` (v4) or `require('@tailwindcss/typography')` (v3). Use `prose` on a container to style all child HTML elements. Size modifiers: `prose-sm` through `prose-2xl`. Dark mode: `prose-invert`. Color: `prose-gray`, `prose-slate`. Override elements: `prose-headings:text-blue-600`, `prose-a:text-indigo-500`.

sections.title: @tailwindcss/forms plugin
sections.body: Provides opinionated form element reset. Install: `npm install @tailwindcss/forms`. Makes inputs, selects, textareas, and checkboxes easy to style with Tailwind utilities. Without it, form elements have inconsistent cross-browser defaults that resist utility class styling. Apply classes directly: `rounded-md border-gray-300 focus:ring-indigo-500`.

sections.title: Creating custom utilities
sections.body: v4 CSS: `@utility name { property: value; }`. Supports nesting, pseudo-elements. All variants (hover, responsive, dark) work automatically. Functional utilities: `@utility tab-* { tab-size: --value(--tab-size-*); }`. v3 JS: use `addUtilities()` in a plugin function. Custom utilities appear alongside built-in ones in the generated stylesheet.

sections.title: Creating custom variants
sections.body: v4 CSS: `@custom-variant name (selector-pattern);`. Example: `@custom-variant hocus (&:hover, &:focus);` enables `hocus:bg-blue-500`. v3 JS: `addVariant('hocus', ['&:hover', '&:focus'])`. Custom variants compose with all other variants: `dark:hocus:bg-blue-700`.

sections.title: v3 JavaScript plugin API
sections.body: Plugin functions receive `addUtilities`, `addComponents`, `addBase`, `addVariant`, `matchUtilities`, and `theme` helpers. `addUtilities` adds classes to the utilities layer. `addComponents` adds to the components layer. `addBase` adds to the base layer. `matchUtilities` creates dynamic utilities from theme values. `addVariant` registers new variant selectors. Wrap in `require('tailwindcss/plugin')(fn)`. Load in v4 with `@plugin` directive.

links.context: Plugins are registered in config (v3) or loaded via @plugin (v4)
Container queries were a plugin in v3, built-in in v4
v4 CSS directives replace many plugin use cases

related.note: Must configure Tailwind before adding plugins
v4 provides CSS-native alternatives to many JS plugins
Plugins extend the available utility classes
